column8:
Well here's the truth:
1. You don't get enough staff to work with in the branch.

2. The workload is distributed as, one person should work for three.

3. They don't have any type of structure for repair and maintenance of IT hardwares installed in branches. If you get any issue you will have to raise a complain and wait for someone's mercy and hope the item to be repaired.

4. The transfer policy has no sense. If you are from north part of our country you can get a posting to a remotest location you can imagine in southern part of our country and vice-versa. If you file a request for transfer, they will state that you have to serve minimum of 3 years in one place but they can transfer you anytime-anyplace.

5. The senior management expects you to work till 8pm everyday. However, on paper it has been stated that systems (batch) should be closed by 6:30 pm, but if you leave at that time it is frowned upon.

6. You will be called to work on public holidays (2nd and 4th Saturday, also Sunday), however, you are entitled for compassionate leave subjected to, if you get the instructions through proper channel. In reality approximately 98% of the instructions are either received orally or WhatsApp for which you can't claim compassionate leave. And, if you dare to not to follow the informal orders, you will feel the heat officially.

7. You are and will be accountable for each and every step you take in branches. No one will come to your help even if it was not intentional (malafide intention). But, if you have used a good quality butter you might escape the biggest blunders you can think of.

8. The CEO and Board of directors have no idea on what is happening on the ground. It seems like they are living in a seperate world where everything is perfect and goes better than the plan itself.

9. The whole bank relies on doing work manually. Even if there is a system generated report they want you to print the whole document, sign it, scan it and then send it to them.

10. And the worst thing is everyone likes to point fingers at others. Nobody wants to take responsibility.

2) The work is challenging if you want to do it properly.
3) You get lots of holidays and nice work life balance (of course this is not true for everyone, if you are at a RO CO or large branch with a lot of pending work then its gonna be difficult)

5)
column8:
There are many cons and some of them are the usual, there is chronic under staffing at branches leading to large number of complaints. A lot of printers and computers are either very old ( I have one with a single core CPU whereas there are i7 all in one PCs too in my branch) or non functional. 

My branch has a lot of people coming in daily for balance enquiry and pass book printing purposes and we don't have a printer working. There are two printers in my branch for printing documents and neither of them work. 

The work has become a lot more organized in the last few years esp with the LAPS etc but there are many operational inefficiencies. 

Everyone is swamped with work. We need more staff. Everyone needs more staff. I wonder why aren't they hiring more staff. 

The main problem with the job is really that it is too secure and that makes it rather difficult to want to switch to something else.

column8:
many...
...working atmosphere is worse than the worst in many branches (almost all old branches)...u will start criticizing GOD from the very first day... 
...present heads of the bank are negative and have allergy with the bank, so criticizes staff heavily on each and every issue.
...heavy work load...
...All the officers of the branch must and should sit till 8 PM (in every big branch- even if there is no work).  Regional office 7 PM, Circle/ Head office 8 PM.
...Heavy partiality (starts from scale 2 level). you will easily observe any where in bank just only after few days of joining bank)
...All good reviews above the bank are just about the branches they have worked, not about the branches you are going to work
...almost all the staff members of the bank (except few) are generally speaks politely with customers (why because, we get terrified with customers. our bank has moto "customer is always correct". officers sitting in regional offices/ circle offices never listens to staff version. They blackmail us on charge sheet/suspension/increment cut/demotion/extension of probation on each and every small customer complaint. Here, almost every week atleast one ruthless customer approaches r.o/c.o/H.o/ombudsman, in each and every branch on small & silly  issues like Pass Book printing rejection due to hardware problem/ Rejection of telling balance in a/c due to server problem/ rejection of issuing loan due to invalid documents ...Only customer's rude and rash talk & behaviour makes us irritated...(ofcourse few of our staff are very irresponsible...)
...torture to every  branch head and every marketing officer (so).
...Our officers calls from r.o/c.o every day to give loans to ineligible persons only because they have approached AGM/DGM/GM.
...Heavy responsibility to each and every issue,,, even scale-1 officers are criticized heavily on a small issue on almost every day... 
...if you really make a mistake due to ignorance you are over,,, If you involve in giving loan to any person without proper documents (even if GM's oral order) you will get charge sheet.
...we all really feel it a pure private job, not a govt organization...
...we all are here only because there is no other choice to us except to continue in this job,

ADVICE TO NEW ASPIRANTS...
